steps.text: First, an institution connects its wallet. The standard flow, no custom wallet needed.
The network operator opens a fresh batch for institutions to join.
Before trading, the institution authorizes an auditor, who gains real, scoped access to decrypt its intents. This is the part no competitor has.
The institution submits an intent, buy four hundred ETH, encrypted on the device. Only a handle reaches the chain, and only the owner can decrypt it.
On the public explorer, everyone else sees just one netted footprint. Never who traded, never how much.
The authorized auditor connects, decrypts exactly what was shared, and exports a wallet-signed compliance report.
